title: Molium nigrum
uses: Molium nigrum, also known as black melic grass, is a type of grass with reported medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. Its roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin. The plant's leaves and stems are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Molium nigrum as a medicinal plant.

Molinia caerulea, also known as Purple Moth, is a perennial grass native to Northern Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Molinia caerulea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
